content:
Supplier Risk Scores: Who Poses the Highest Risk at Pendle?

Key Findings

  • Pendle Borough Council's analysed spending totalled £129.1M across five financial years.
  • The council engaged with 3,019 unique suppliers during this period.
  • Among the suppliers identified for review within the 'top risk' category, all currently hold a composite risk score of 0.
  • Liberata UK Ltd received the largest spend among these identified suppliers, totalling £16.7M.

Understanding Supplier Risk at Pendle

Pendle Borough Council, a district council in Lancashire, manages significant public funds. AI DOGE has analysed the council's published spending data, covering the financial years 2021/22 to 2025/26. This extensive dataset provides insight into how the council allocates its budget and the organisations it works with. Over this five-year period, the total spend analysed reached £129.1M. This considerable sum was distributed among a wide network of partners, with the council engaging 3,019 unique suppliers.

Understanding the risk associated with suppliers is a crucial aspect of public finance management. Supplier risk scoring helps councils identify potential vulnerabilities, ensure value for money, and maintain service continuity. While a comprehensive risk assessment involves many factors, AI DOGE's analysis focuses on identifying suppliers flagged for review. For Pendle Borough Council, our analysis identified a specific group of suppliers for closer examination. It is important to note that, based on the current data, all these identified suppliers hold a composite risk score of 0.

Key Suppliers and Their Risk Profiles

Our analysis pinpointed several suppliers within the 'top risk' category, meaning they were identified for review based on specific criteria. However, their calculated composite risk scores are all 0. This indicates that, according to the current methodology, these suppliers do not present an elevated risk to Pendle Borough Council. Despite their low risk scores, some of these suppliers account for substantial portions of the council's expenditure. Examining these relationships is vital for transparency and accountability.

The following table details the suppliers identified in the 'top risk' category, their individual spend with Pendle Borough Council, and their current composite risk score of 0:

Supplier Name Spend Risk Score
Liberata UK Ltd £16.7M 0
PENDLE LEISURE LTD £6.7M 0
ORCHARD RECYCLING SERVICE £661K 0
STANLEY BROTHERS (TIPPERS) LTD £138K 0
ASPEN VALLEY KENNELS & CATTERY £55K 0
JOHN MACADAM & SON (RESCUE) LTD £30K 0
S M SHEPHERD BUILDING CONTRACTORS £20K 0
VISION TECHNIQUES UK LTD £19K 0
MW LANDSCAPE & DESIGN LTD £19K 0
R J CLOUGH & SON LTD £18K 0

Significant Spend with Low-Risk Suppliers

Among the suppliers identified for review, Liberata UK Ltd stands out with a total spend of £16.7M. This represents a significant portion of the council's overall expenditure across the five-year period. Despite this substantial financial relationship, Liberata UK Ltd holds a composite risk score of 0. This indicates that, based on the available data and risk assessment methodology, the council's engagement with this major supplier is not currently flagged as high-risk.

Another key supplier, PENDLE LEISURE LTD, also shows a considerable spend of £6.7M. Like Liberata UK Ltd, this organisation also has a composite risk score of 0. Other suppliers with notable spending figures include ORCHARD RECYCLING SERVICE at £661K and STANLEY BROTHERS (TIPPERS) LTD at £138K. The consistent risk score of 0 across all these identified suppliers suggests that Pendle Borough Council's current supplier relationships, particularly with these major partners, are assessed as low risk.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Pendle Borough Council about their supplier risk assessment processes and criteria.
  • Attend council committee meetings, such as those related to finance or procurement, as they are open to the public.
  • Check the council's published contracts register for details on agreements with suppliers like Liberata UK Ltd and PENDLE LEISURE LTD.
  • Write to your local councillor about the council's spending with specific suppliers and how supplier risk is managed.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Pendle Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Pendle Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
